message.content:
Test file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-17_01-11-17_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation files:
  /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-17_01-11-17_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts
  /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-17_01-11-17_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/cli.ts
Failing test: "claim referencing item not in policy causes non-zero exit"
Current error: status is 0 (success), expected non-zero
Passing tests so far: 29

The scenario: policy contains a sword; claim references a damage entry with itemType "amulet" (not in the policy). The CLI must:
- exit non-zero
- write to stderr

Also per spec: if `damages` array has MORE entries of a type than the policy covers, that's also an error (e.g., 2 sword damages but only 1 sword insured). But the current test only exercises the simpler case of unknown item type for a damage.

Implement minimal validation in handleClaim (cli.ts) — verify each damage entry's itemType exists in the policy items. If a damage type isn't covered, throw. Keep simple. Also consider that the existing test "deductible applies per damaged item: sword 500 + amulet 300" has each damage type once and policy has each item once — that should still work.

But also: existing claim test "successive claims: remaining cap decreases" only has one sword in policy, and damages contain one sword — works.

Be mindful: do not break the existing test "deductible applies per damaged item: sword 500 + amulet 300 -> payout 600" which has TWO damage entries (sword, amulet) and TWO items in policy (sword, amulet).

The simplest validation: for each damage entry, the policy must contain an item with matching type. Possibly also count-matched (no more damages of a type than policy items of that type). Pick whichever is minimal to pass the test.

Run `pnpm test:unit:basic` to verify all 30 tests pass.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the failing test and implement the minimal validation needed.
Now I'll add minimal validation to handleClaim. The simplest check: each damage entry's itemType must exist in the policy items.

content.text:
All 30 tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
- Implementation: Added a minimal check in `handleClaim` that scans `step.incident.damages` for any entry whose `itemType` is not present in `policy.items`; if found, throws an error which `main`'s catch handler converts into a non-zero exit and stderr write.
- Result: All tests now pass (30 passing, 1 todo).
- Approach: Used `Array.find` plus `Array.some` for the simplest possible per-damage coverage check — no count-matching logic, no additional helpers, no refactoring. The existing test with two damages (sword + amulet) still passes because both types exist in the policy's items.

Files changed:
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-17_01-11-17_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/cli.ts

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
